terms.definition: A feedforward neural network with one or more hidden layers between input and output, each applying a linear transformation followed by a nonlinear activation so the full model can represent non-linear decision boundaries.
Common abbreviation for multilayer perceptron — a dense feedforward stack used as a baseline classifier or regressor before specialized architectures.
In an MLP, any intermediate layer between the input presentation and the output head whose activations are not the final prediction but internal features recombined by later layers.
A classification surface that cannot be drawn as a single hyperplane in input space; MLPs form such boundaries by composing piecewise linear regions via hidden units and activations.
An MLP where activations flow strictly from input to output with no cycles — the standard fully connected pattern implemented as repeated Linear → activation blocks.
The final linear layer (plus optional task activation) that maps the last hidden representation to predictions — class logits, probabilities, or a regression scalar.
